subtopics.title: Concepts of Simple Harmonic Motion
common_misconceptions: SHM means the motion is simple (it means sinusoidal)
Acceleration is constant in SHM (it varies with displacement)
All oscillatory motion is SHM (only if restoring force ∝ displacement)
subtopics.teaching_note: Key equations: a = -ω²x (acceleration proportional to displacement, opposite direction). v = ±ω√(A²-x²). Period of simple pendulum: T = 2π√(L/g). Period of loaded spring: T = 2π√(m/k). ω = 2πf = 2π/T. Maximum velocity: v_max = Aω (at equilibrium). Maximum acceleration: a_max = Aω² (at extremes). Energy in SHM: KE = ½mω²(A²-x²), PE = ½mω²x², Total = ½mω²A² (constant).

subtopics.title: Electric Field and Potential
common_misconceptions: Electric field is a scalar (it's a vector)
Electric potential is the same as potential energy
subtopics.teaching_note: Key equations: E = F/q = kQ/r² (field intensity, N/C or V/m). V = kQ/r (potential, scalar, V). For parallel plates: E = V/d (uniform). Force on charge: F = qE. Work done: W = qV. Potential difference: V_AB = V_A - V_B. Electron volt: 1 eV = 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ J (energy gained by electron accelerated through 1V). Field lines: start from positive, end on negative, never cross, denser where field is stronger.

subtopics.title: Capacitors and Capacitance
common_misconceptions: Capacitance depends on charge and voltage (NO, C = Q/V is definition, C depends on geometry)
Capacitors store charge (they store energy in electric field)
Series: C_total = C₁ + C₂ (NO, that's parallel)
subtopics.teaching_note: Key equations: C = Q/V (definition, unit: farad, F). Parallel plate: C = εA/d where ε = ε₀εᵣ. ε₀ = 8.85×10⁻¹² F/m. Energy stored: E = ½QV = ½CV² = ½Q²/C. Series: 1/C = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂. Parallel: C = C₁ + C₂. Time constant: τ = RC (charging/discharging). Charging: q = Q(1-e^(-t/RC)). Discharging: q = Qe^(-t/RC). Applications: camera flash, smoothing circuits, tuning circuits, energy storage.
